programmes.show_description: Ancient Civilisations: Nubian pyramids reveal the rise of female power in Ancient Kush, while satellite images uncover secrets of an ancient Puebloan site in New Mexico. (S1, ep 5)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 3)
Giorgio Tsoukalos takes a look back at the most compelling investigations of all, including events that he believes represent real encounters with extraterrestrial visitors.
Deadeye Driver: The swamp heats up as Daniel and Big Tee face off against trespassers. Meanwhile, an old-timer teaches Troy some new tricks. (S11, ep 7)
Boiling Point: As a heat wave grips the Everglades, Bruce and Zak call in canine reinforcements to sniff out serpents hiding from the rising temperature. (S2, ep 7)
Hurricane Ashley: Les and Seth are forced to face up to the fact that Ashley's attitude is costing them business. A serial killer groupie brings in some scary paraphernalia. (S8, ep 9)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 23)
Northwich: David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. But will the locals take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction? (S10, ep 3)
Music star George Canyon has a request the guys can't refuse while Scott makes a pricey custom order at an Ontario motorcycle garage. (S4, ep 1)
Henry Cole visits a farm sale and comes away with a vintage tractor. However, he comes to regret their decision. (S2, ep 8)
I Love Lupe!: The women outnumber the men in Rosamond as Dusty and his girlfriend discover a stockpile of vintage TV memorabilia. (S15, ep 5)
Etrog, Lupe?: Lancaster turns into Ivy's honeypot, Lisa plans to use her brain when selecting lockers and Dusty and Tollbooth Lupe do a mitzvah. (S15, ep 6)
To Catch A Thief - Part 1: Les is horrified to find jewellery scattered on the shop floor and the business's security system disabled. Can the Golds catch their thief? (S8, ep 12)
To Catch A Thief - Part 2: The Golds set up an elaborate sting to catch the thief that's in their midst. Who is it? And do they have enough evidence to involve the police? (S8, ep 13)
Appetite for Pawnstruction: A Guns N' Roses-mad collector box rocks into the shop. Will Corey end up in Paradise City or out in the cold November rain?
One Man Army: At the midway point of alligator season, Troy makes an urgent plea. Jacob and Don fish the deep waters of West Grand Lake where the biggest bull gators lurk. (S11, ep 8)
All Hands on Deck: All five hunters band together on the northern edge of the Everglades to stop the invasion of serpents into a critical bird sanctuary. But is it too late? (S2, ep 8)
Music star George Canyon has a request the guys can't refuse while Scott makes a pricey custom order at an Ontario motorcycle garage. (S4, ep 1)
Vending, Vidi, Vici!: The buyers head to Palm Springs in search of higher-end goods. Kenny tries to amp up the profit on a unit and Rene sees quick potential where others don't. (S15, ep 7)
Parasol Pals: Dusty and Lupe steal a no-brainer locker, which pays off handsomely. Meanwhile, Darrell taxes himself - which almost puts him in storage jail. (S15, ep 8)
Henry Cole heads solo to an automobilia and vintage toy auction, before his team regroups and heads to Newark autojumble. (S2, ep 9)
Return of the Egyptian Gods: A British woman in the 20th century stunned Egyptologists by accurately locating structures and artefacts. Perhaps she had an otherworldly agenda? (S14, ep 20)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 5)
Rick and Chum's Wild Ride: A vintage Harley T-shirt makes Corey go wild, while Rick and Chum head to California to take a peek at a mind-blowing Disney collection.
I Love Lupe!: The women outnumber the men in Rosamond as Dusty and his girlfriend discover a stockpile of vintage TV memorabilia. (S15, ep 5)
Etrog, Lupe?: Lancaster turns into Ivy's honeypot, Lisa plans to use her brain when selecting lockers and Dusty and Tollbooth Lupe do a mitzvah. (S15, ep 6)
Northwich: David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. But will the locals take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction? (S10, ep 4)
Chicago O'hare Airport: Expert Chris Rutkowski, home to the largest UFO research archive, sets the scene and explains an unexplained event. (S1, ep 3)
Physical Evidence: Physical evidence of alien activity has been hidden from the public for years - but it is starting to be revealed. (S1, ep 11)
Close Encounters: Close encounters come in many different forms, from sightings to abductions. Meet the real people who have experienced alien life up close. (S1, ep 13)
California: California is home to numerous sightings of unidentified flying objects. The size of the state is a recipe for explained and unexplained sightings. (S1, ep 8)
Behind The Gates: Hot, Hot, Hot Spots: Matty Blake and the team break down how the phenomena, and the locations where they happen on Skinwalker Ranch, may all be connected. (S5, ep 25)
Behind The Gates: Phenomenal Connections: Dr Travis Taylor reviews evidence the team has documented, suggesting they may be close to revealing the secret of Skinwalker Ranch. (S5, ep 26)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 4)
Return of the Egyptian Gods: A British woman in the 20th century stunned Egyptologists by accurately locating structures and artefacts. Perhaps she had an otherworldly agenda? (S14, ep 20)
One Man Army: At the midway point of alligator season, Troy makes an urgent plea. Jacob and Don fish the deep waters of West Grand Lake where the biggest bull gators lurk. (S11, ep 8)
All Hands on Deck: All five hunters band together on the northern edge of the Everglades to stop the invasion of serpents into a critical bird sanctuary. But is it too late? (S2, ep 8)
Les' Mojo: After a run of bad purchases, can Les prove to his staff that he's still got it? Or has he lost his touch for good? (S8, ep 11)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 24)
Northwich: David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. But will the locals take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction? (S10, ep 4)
The guys find a barber shop shrine to the Toronto Maple Leafs, Sheldon challenges Scott to a 15-minute pick-a-thon and a new employee joins the Calgary warehouse staff. (S4, ep 2)
Henry Cole heads solo to an automobilia and vintage toy auction, before his team regroups and heads to Newark autojumble. (S2, ep 9)
Vending, Vidi, Vici!: The buyers head to Palm Springs in search of higher-end goods. Kenny tries to amp up the profit on a unit and Rene sees quick potential where others don't. (S15, ep 7)
Parasol Pals: Dusty and Lupe steal a no-brainer locker, which pays off handsomely. Meanwhile, Darrell taxes himself - which almost puts him in storage jail. (S15, ep 8)
Frisky Business: Follow the quirky day-to day operations of American Jewelry and Loan, a family-owned and operated pawn shop in Detroit, Michigan. (S9, ep 1)
Short Staffed: With the shop short staffed, Seth struggles to stay on the ball. With each new high drama customer, he gets closer and closer to his breaking point. (S9, ep 2)
Wreck it Rick: Rick and Chum kick it with a rare pair of Pumas from 1968. Plus, the gang are stunned by some of Marie Curie's lab notes and a Post Malone guitar.
Airboat Armada: Troy brings together a crew to infiltrate Cow Island. Ronnie and Ashley assist the Landrys with an armada of airboats. Brock and Aaron chase a big beast. (S11, ep 9)
New Blood: Troy Landry teaches partner Pickle Wheat the ins and outs of python hunting. Hunters Bill Booth and Tes Lee attempt to put a dent in the growing snake population. (S3, ep 1)
The guys find a barber shop shrine to the Toronto Maple Leafs, Sheldon challenges Scott to a 15-minute pick-a-thon and a new employee joins the Calgary warehouse staff. (S4, ep 2)
The Way of Gam Gam: While in Santa Ana, Lisa channels her grandmother in trying to turn a profit. Meanwhile, Brandi deals with the most frustrating day ever. (S15, ep 9)
Four Bidders and a Funeral: Lisa comes to Montebello with a clear mind, intent on letting the lockers speak to her. Ivy lets his son navigate his way to an interesting unit. (S15, ep 10)
Henry, Allen and Guy finally get their Buick Roadmaster, cruise to a Newbury car show, and do some wheeling and dealing. (S2, ep 10)
UFO Hotspots: Studies show 70% of UFO sightings occur in 'hotspots', where unusual phenomena is regularly reported. Is there something about these places that attracts visitors? (S15, ep 1)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 6)
Appetite for Pawnstruction: A Guns N' Roses-mad collector box rocks into the shop. Will Corey end up in Paradise City or out in the cold November rain?

Parasol Pals: Dusty and Lupe steal a no-brainer locker, which pays off handsomely. Meanwhile, Darrell taxes himself - which almost puts him in storage jail. (S15, ep 8)
Welwyn Garden City: Antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. (S10, ep 5)
Do You Know Who I Am?: An impatient SUV driver in Oregon attacks a cyclist at a red light and gets a serious comeuppance. A driver chases down a fleeing driver who hit her. (S1, ep 5)
All Keyed up: A distracted police officer in Missouri strikes a bicyclist with his vehicle and woman is caught red-handed keying another person's car in Louisiana. (S1, ep 6)
A river raft guide spots an infamous cryptid along the Colorado River and a UK man taking out his trash appears to pick up a ghostly hitchhiker. (S8, ep 11)
An otherworldly figure walks across a Pennsylvania woman's front lawn and a haunted doll gets physical with its visitors in England. (S8, ep 12)
